key_topics: Proportional relationships: constant of proportionality, graphs, equations
Percents: percent change, markup, markdown, commission
Rational number operations: all four with positive and negative fractions/decimals
Expressions: expanding, factoring using distributive property
Solving equations: multi-step, variables on both sides
Inequalities: solving and graphing
Geometry: scale drawings, constructions with compass and straightedge
Circles: circumference and area
Surface area and volume: composite figures
Probability: simple and compound events, tree diagrams
Statistics: sampling, central tendency, variability, comparing distributions
Introduction to functions: definition, notation, input-output
competences: Solve multi-step equations with rational coefficients
Calculate percent change in real-world contexts
Compare statistical distributions using measures of centre and spread
Understand the concept of a function
typical_mistakes: Errors solving equations with fractions (not multiplying both sides)
Confusing percent decrease with percent of original
Not listing all outcomes in tree diagrams

key_topics: Reading: canonical American and world literature
Shakespeare: Romeo and Juliet or A Midsummer Night's Dream
Greek epic poetry: The Odyssey (selected books)
Literary analysis: extended essay on a complete novel
Rhetorical analysis: SOAPS (Speaker, Occasion, Audience, Purpose, Subject)
Writing: literary analysis essays with textual evidence
Research paper: 3-5 pages with 5+ sources, MLA format
Creative writing: short story with literary techniques
Grammar: sentence variety for stylistic effect
Advanced vocabulary: connotation, diction, syntax
Speaking: Socratic seminar, formal debate
competences: Write extended literary analysis essays with embedded textual evidence
Perform SOAPS rhetorical analysis on speeches and essays
Complete a research paper with proper MLA citations
Read and analyse Shakespeare with contextual understanding
typical_mistakes: Summarising plot in analytical essays (not analysing)
Incomplete SOAPS analyses (missing purpose or audience)
Mixing MLA and APA citation styles
Difficulty with Elizabethan English in Shakespeare

key_topics: World History: Age of Revolution — American, French, Haitian
Napoleon and the Napoleonic Wars
19th Century Europe: nationalism, industrialisation, imperialism
The Scramble for Africa: European colonialism
Asian civilisations: Japan (Meiji), China (Qing), India (British Raj)
Latin American independence movements: Bolívar, San Martín
World Geography: comparing world regions using data
Human Geography: population, urbanisation, migration
competences: Compare the American and French Revolutions
Explain the causes and consequences of European imperialism in Africa
Trace Latin American independence movements chronologically
Analyse demographic data for world regions
typical_mistakes: Confusing the causes of WWI with 19th century imperialism
Not understanding the legacy of colonialism in contemporary Africa

key_topics: Biology: cell biology — cell cycle, mitosis, meiosis
Genetics: Mendelian genetics, sex-linked traits, incomplete dominance
Evolution: evidence (fossil, anatomical, molecular), natural selection
Chemistry: atomic theory, Bohr model, electron configuration
The Periodic Table: groups, periods, properties, families
Chemical bonding: ionic and covalent bonds
Chemical equations: balancing equations
Physics: motion — velocity, acceleration, Newton's Laws
Work, power, and energy: calculations
competences: Explain mitosis and meiosis and their roles
Solve genetics problems including sex-linked traits
Apply Newton's Laws to motion problems
typical_mistakes: Confusing mitosis and meiosis (number of divisions, purpose)
Errors balancing equations (changing subscripts instead of coefficients)
